blurb:
Rreal Tacos is one of those places the neighborhood keeps to itself but probably should not. The vibe is loud and fun, the service is attentive, and the food actually lives up to what people say about it. Great for a group dinner or a casual night out when you want good food and a couple of good drinks.

Start with the Esquite before anything else. For drinks, the Pineapple Guava pitcher and the Passion Fruit pitcher are both worth ordering, especially if you are sharing with the table. They are refreshing and well-balanced without being too sweet.

They are open until midnight every night except Sunday, so it is also a solid late-night option if you need one.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE #506, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This one is a real hidden gem on Allgood Road. It's a Mexican grocery store with a taqueria in the back, and the food coming out of that kitchen is genuinely excellent. Don't let the setup fool you. The people who know about it keep coming back.

The al pastor is the move here. It's well seasoned, not too crispy, and cooked exactly right. The bean and cheese pupusas are 10 out of 10, and the chicken plate with rice and beans is worth ordering too. The beans are made from scratch, not from a can, and you can tell. The tortas are great as well if you want something handheld.

This is a cash-and-carry kind of spot, cheap and fast. Perfect for lunch or a quick weeknight dinner when you want real food without a big bill.
address: 786 Allgood Rd NE, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
Laredos is our go-to on Sandy Plains when we want solid Mexican food without a lot of fuss. The inside is colorful and festive, and it has that cantina feel that makes it work for a family dinner or a casual night out with friends. The margaritas are a big draw and worth ordering.

They do all the Mexican classics well. It's the kind of place where everyone at the table finds something they want, and the portions are generous for the price. Good for a weeknight when you don't want to cook but also don't want to spend a lot.

They're open every day until at least 10pm, which is handy when the dinner decision happens late.
address: 1860 Sandy Plains Rd, Marietta, GA 30066, USA
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Royal Spice on Hammond Drive is a good go-to when you're craving Indian food and want a spot that actually takes care of you. The staff is patient and happy to answer questions about the menu, which is helpful if you're still figuring out what you like. It's a comfortable sit-down place, not too loud, good for a weeknight dinner or a low-key date night.

The entrees and appetizers both get solid marks from people who eat here regularly. If you have questions about spice levels or ingredients, just ask. The servers are used to it and will steer you in the right direction.

Note that they close between lunch and dinner on weekdays, so there's a gap between 2:30pm and 4:30pm. Worth keeping in mind if you're planning around a specific time.
address: 220 Hammond Dr #302, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
This little spot on Roswell Road brings something you don't see much around here: a genuine Brazilian açaí bar with a warm, welcoming vibe. The self-serve açaí bowl setup means you build it exactly how you want it, adding as many toppings as you like. It's a great after-school stop with the kids or a lighter option when you want something refreshing.

Beyond the açaí, they also have crepes and coxinha, a classic Brazilian fried snack. The staff is friendly and clearly takes pride in the place. With a 4.9 rating and nearly perfect reviews, it's one of those hidden gems worth seeking out.

They're closed on Tuesdays, but open until 10pm the rest of the week.
address: 2145 Roswell Rd Suite 110, Marietta, GA 30062, USA

blurb:
SATE on Roswell Road is a small spot with a big reputation around here. The owner is hands-on and genuinely passionate about Vietnamese food. Reviewers mention he'll actually walk you through the menu and explain how things are made, which makes it feel nothing like a typical quick-stop lunch place. Good for a casual weekday meal or introducing someone to Vietnamese food for the first time.

The pho is the thing to order. They make it the traditional way, and people who have been going for years still talk about it. If you've never had pho before, this is a great place to start.

Prices are very reasonable and it's open every day, so it's easy to work into your regular lunch rotation.
address: 5980 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Canton Cooks is right on Roswell Road and it's been around long enough that plenty of people in the area have a standing order. It's a casual Chinese place, nothing dressy, good for a weeknight dinner or takeout when you don't feel like cooking. The menu goes beyond the usual American Chinese fare, which is part of why it has regulars.

The lobster in black bean sauce and the BBQ duck are the things that set it apart from other spots nearby. If you've only ever done the standard takeout items, those two are worth trying. They also do hot pot if you're in the mood for something more interactive.

Open every day until 10pm, so it works well for late dinners too.
address: 5984 Roswell Rd NE, Atlanta, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Los Bravos has been a go-to for a lot of East Cobb families for years, and the food from the kitchen is still the reason to go. It's an easy pick for a weeknight dinner out or a casual weekend meal when everyone wants something reliable and affordable.

The food gets solid marks from long-time regulars, and the portions are generous for the price. Just be aware that the margaritas have gotten pricey compared to other spots nearby. The kitchen is the draw here, not the bar.

They're open seven days a week with late hours on Thursday through Saturday, which makes it convenient for a late dinner.
hours: Monday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 10:30 PM, Sunday: 11:00 AM – 10:00 PM

blurb:
Sunnyside Pizzeria is one of those places you end up recommending to everyone who moves to the area. It's in a strip center off Sandy Springs Circle, but the pizza is genuinely good enough that people drive for it. Great for a Friday night with the family or picking up a pie on the way home.

Start with the Hot Honey Lemon Pepper Chicken Wings if you're going in person. They're sweet, a little spicy, and people rave about them. On the pizza side, the Hot Honey options get a lot of love from regulars. The Caesar Salad is also a solid order if you want something fresh on the side.

They're closed on Mondays, so plan around that.
address: 220 Sandy Springs Cir Suite 149, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA

blurb:
Ray's on the River is the place you take someone when you really want to impress them. It sits right on the Chattahoochee in Sandy Springs, and the view alone is worth the trip. This is date night territory, a special anniversary, or a nice dinner with out-of-town family who wants to see what Atlanta does well.

The menu focuses on Southern seafood, and the setting is genuinely beautiful without feeling stuffy. With over 11,000 reviews and a 4.6 rating, it's held up its reputation for a long time. Budget accordingly, this is a splurge dinner.

Sunday hours are split, so check before you go if you're planning a late Sunday meal.
address: 6700 Powers Ferry Rd NW, Sandy Springs, GA 30339, USA

blurb:
Heirloom Market BBQ is a small, no-frills spot near the Akers Mill area, and it has a serious following for good reason. The twist here is that it's BBQ with a Korean influence, so you get familiar smoked meats with a little something different going on with the flavors. There's one communal table inside, so it's more of a grab-and-go situation than a sit-down dinner experience. Perfect for a casual lunch or picking up food on the way home.

The BBQ is the focus, and with nearly 3,000 reviews at 4.7 stars, people clearly keep coming back. It's a great spot to try if you want something a step beyond typical barbecue without going far from home.

They're closed Sundays and Mondays, and they only serve until 8pm Tuesday through Saturday, so plan ahead.
address: 2243 Akers Mill Rd SE, Atlanta, GA 30339, USA
hours: Monday: Closed, Tuesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM – 8:00 PM, Sunday: Closed

blurb:
If you're looking for a solid breakfast or lunch spot that doesn't make you wait forever, J. Christopher's in Sandy Springs is worth knowing about. It's a daytime-only place, open every day until 2pm, so it's good for a lazy weekend morning or a weekday lunch when you want something a little more than fast food. The vibe is casual and comfortable, nothing fancy.

They do classic breakfast stuff well. Omelets are a safe bet, and they also do burgers for the lunch crowd. They run seasonal specials too, so there's usually something a little different if you're a regular.

Just remember they close at 2pm every day, so don't show up mid-afternoon expecting to grab a bite.
address: 227 Sandy Springs Pl NE, Sandy Springs, GA 30328, USA
